calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To May-13-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Court'S Orders And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph Lake, of Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4217, for defendants;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion To Enforce The Court's Orders And Request For Sanctions is granted. The motion to enforce the Court's prior orders and for sanctions was unopposed. The Court's prior order to compel responses to defendant's written discovery stated that plaintiff would face a terminating sanction if he failed to comply with the Court's order. (Order Granting Defendant's Motion To Enforce the Court's Order and Request for Discovery, dated August 21, 2018, Lake Decl. Exh. I.) The record indicates that plaintiff was present at the hearing that led to this Order, and that this prior Order was served on plaintiff. Under the circumstances, the Court believes that a terminating sanction is appropriate. (Lake Decl. at 12, 13.). Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: J. Timothy Nardell; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2018 Set For Trial On Nov-19-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Court Orders And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James A. Gayapa, appearing in Pro Per, Telephone (415)420-2577;
Joseph Lake, City Attorney, appearing on behalf of City and County of San Francisco, Telephone (415)554-4217;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Enforce The Court Orders And Request For Sanctions.

Defendant's motion for an order to enforce sanctions is unopposed and granted to the extent plaintiff shall have twenty days from the date of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry by both mail and email to serve code compliant verified responses, without objection, to all discovery requests which are the subject of the motion. This is the final opportunity for plaintiff to comply with the two previous Orders of this Court. If the plaintiff fails to comply he shall have no further opportunities to respond and the Court will entertain a motion to dismiss his action.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
